hint: gu before é to keep hard /g/

front: it pleased me / I liked it
it was pleasing me / I was liking it
I was cooking when the phone rang.
I was talking on the phone when Maria arrived.
back: Hablaba por teléfono cuando María llegó.

front: It seems good to me. (Me parece bien)
It was seeming good to me.
When I saw it, I didn't like it.
back: Cuando lo vi, no me gustó.

front: It was seeming good, but I didn't like it.
back: Me parecía bien, pero no me gustó.

front: I was cooking when Maria arrived.
I was speaking on the phone.
It seemed good to me, but I didn't like it.
back: Me pareció bien, pero no me gustó.

front: It seems good to me, but I don't know.
back: Me parece bien, pero no sé.

front: It didn't seem good to me.
It wasn't seeming good to me.
I didn't like it when I saw it.
back: No me gustó cuando lo vi.

hint: dar takes -er/-ir endings in point-past · short, no accent
build verb first, lo in front
le+lo would crash → se · purely phonetic
clarifying *a* tag · always REPEAT the little word
keep le AND tag a él · always repeat
para → no repetition, no extra little word
no L-L crash with me · plural direct -los

front: I bought it because of you.

front: I don't remember if I gave it to him.
back: No me acuerdo si se lo di.

front: I don't remember if I gave it to him or her.
back: No me acuerdo si se lo di a él o a ella.

front: We don't remember if we gave it to him.
back: No nos acordamos si se lo dimos.

front: We don't remember if we gave it to them (fem).
